title: ICT Coaching vs FTMO — R2F
seoTitle: ICT Trading Coaching vs FTMO: Is Mentorship Worth It? | R2F Trading
seoDescription: Comparing ICT trading coaching vs FTMO? See why personal mentorship with R2F Trading beats going solo through a prop firm challenge. Start learning smarter.
headline: ICT Trading Coaching vs FTMO: Stop Funding Losses, Start Learning First
subheadline: Most traders fail FTMO challenges not because of discipline — but because they never truly understood the ICT concepts they were trading. R2F Trading's personal mentorship fixes that root problem before you risk a single dollar on a prop firm evaluation.

keyPoints.title: What This Comparison Really Means
keyPoints.text: FTMO is a prop firm that funds traders after they pass a performance challenge — it is not a coaching program. ICT trading coaching through R2F Trading, led by Harvest Wright, teaches you the actual methodology: order blocks, fair value gaps, liquidity, and market structure. These are two completely different tools, and confusing them is costing traders thousands in failed evaluations.

keyPoints.title: Why It Matters to Your Bottom Line
keyPoints.text: The average trader attempts multiple FTMO challenges before passing — each attempt costing $155 to $540+. Without a solid ICT foundation, you are essentially paying to gamble. Personal mentorship with R2F Trading builds the conceptual clarity and execution discipline that dramatically increases your probability of passing a prop firm challenge on the first or second attempt, saving you money and months of frustration.

keyPoints.title: How to Use Both Tools Together
keyPoints.text: The smartest path is sequential: invest in ICT coaching first to master the methodology, then pursue FTMO or another prop firm once your edge is proven on a demo or small live account. R2F Trading's mentorship program walks you through ICT concepts in real market conditions with Harvest's direct feedback, so by the time you sit for an evaluation, your strategy is battle-tested — not theoretical.

keyPoints.title: The Mistake That Kills Most Prop Traders
keyPoints.text: The most common mistake is entering an FTMO challenge after watching YouTube videos and assuming you understand ICT well enough to perform under pressure. Surface-level knowledge collapses when real evaluation rules — drawdown limits, consistency requirements, profit targets — are added. Without a mentor correcting your bias identification, entry timing, and risk model in real time, small misunderstandings compound into blown challenges and empty wallets.

keyPoints.title: Your Next Step: Mentorship Before the Challenge
keyPoints.text: If you are serious about getting funded, book a discovery call with Harvest Wright at r2ftrading.com before your next prop firm attempt. R2F Trading's ICT coaching program is built specifically for traders who want to earn consistent funding — not just pass a single challenge. Get the foundation right, and the evaluation becomes the easy part.
